Title: GATA and Phytochrome Interacting Factor Transcription Factors Regulate Light-Induced Vindoline Biosynthesis in Catharanthus roseus

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: precursor of the anticancer alkaloids vincristine and vinblastine
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Light inducible; expression gradually increases under white light and peaks around 24 h; induced by red light and reversed by far-red; preferentially expressed in aerial parts
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Light inducible; expression gradually increases under white light and peaks around 24 h; induced by red light and reversed by far-red; preferentially expressed in aerial parts
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with promotion of vindoline biosynthesis when induced by light/CrGATA1 and repression by CrPIF1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression profiling; CrGATA1/CrPIF1 overexpression and VIGS; promoter transactivation assay
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native T16H2 promoter in GUS reporter assay
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: coexpression analysis using transcriptome resource SRA030483
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: white light; red light; far-red light; dark
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful as part of coordinated vindoline-pathway engineering, especially with CrGATA1 activation or CrPIF1 repression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T16H2 is one of five vindoline-pathway genes induced by light in seedlings. 2. Its promoter is activated by CrGATA1 and repressed by CrPIF1. 3. Its expression tracks with vindoline accumulation under light-regulated conditions.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although regulated in assays, this paper focused primarily on transcriptional regulators rather than biochemical characterization of T16H2.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: precursor of the anticancer alkaloids vincristine and vinblastine
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Light inducible; peaks around 24 h under white light; preferentially expressed in aerial parts
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Light inducible; peaks around 24 h under white light; preferentially expressed in aerial parts
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports vindoline biosynthesis and is positively associated with light-induced pathway activation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; CrGATA1/CrPIF1 perturbation; promoter transactivation assay
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native T3O promoter in GUS reporter assay
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: coexpression analysis using transcriptome resource SRA030483
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate target in multigene engineering of vindoline production
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T3O is light inducible in seedlings. 2. Its promoter is transactivated by CrGATA1. 3. Its transcript decreases in CrGATA1-VIGS leaves and is repressed by CrPIF1 overexpression.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression did not increase significantly in CrGATA1 overexpression seedlings, indicating additional regulatory constraints.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: precursor of the anticancer alkaloids vincristine and vinblastine
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Light inducible; highest at 48 h of light treatment; preferentially expressed in aerial parts
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Light inducible; highest at 48 h of light treatment; preferentially expressed in aerial parts
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports vindoline biosynthesis and is positively associated with light-induced pathway activation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; CrGATA1/CrPIF1 perturbation; promoter transactivation assay
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native T3R promoter in GUS reporter assay
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: coexpression analysis using transcriptome resource SRA030483
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate target in pathway stacking for vindoline engineering
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T3R is a light-induced vindoline-pathway gene. 2. Its promoter is activated by CrGATA1. 3. Silencing CrGATA1 reduces T3R expression, while CrPIF1 overexpression represses it.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression response differs from some other pathway genes, with strongest induction at 48 h rather than 24 h.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: precursor of the anticancer alkaloids vincristine and vinblastine
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Light inducible; peaks around 24 h under white light; preferentially expressed in aerial parts
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Light inducible; peaks around 24 h under white light; preferentially expressed in aerial parts
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports vindoline biosynthesis and responds strongly to light and CrGATA1 activation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; promoter-GUS light assay; CrGATA1 overexpression/VIGS; promoter transactivation; site-directed mutagenesis of GATA/GATC motifs
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native D4H promoter in GUS reporter assay
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: coexpression analysis using transcriptome resource SRA030483
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate promoter/gene module for light-responsive engineering of vindoline biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D4H is a classic light-responsive vindoline-pathway gene. 2. CrGATA1 strongly transactivates the D4H promoter. 3. Two GATC motifs in the D4H promoter are critical for CrGATA1-mediated activation, whereas tested GATA motifs are dispensable.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: D4H expression was not significantly changed in CrGATA1-VIGS and was not clearly induced in the specific red-light assay window, suggesting temporal or compensatory regulation.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: precursor of the anticancer alkaloids vincristine and vinblastine
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly light inducible; peaks around 24 h under white light; induced by red light and reversed by far-red; preferentially expressed in aerial parts
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly light inducible; peaks around 24 h under white light; induced by red light and reversed by far-red; preferentially expressed in aerial parts
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively associated with vindoline accumulation and targeted by both CrGATA1 activation and CrPIF1 repression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; promoter-GUS light assay; CrGATA1/CrPIF1 overexpression and VIGS; promoter transactivation; mutagenesis of DAT PBE-box
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native DAT promoter in GUS reporter assay
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: coexpression analysis using transcriptome resource SRA030483
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: white light; red light; far-red light; dark
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: important terminal-step target for improving vindoline accumulation; promoter engineering at the PBE-box/GATA-responsive context may tune light responsiveness
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DAT is one of the most clearly light-responsive vindoline genes. 2. CrGATA1 activates the DAT promoter, while CrPIF1 represses it in the dark through a PBE-box. 3. DAT transcript changes correlate well with vindoline accumulation in regulator perturbation assays.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Frequently highlighted as a key light-responsive vindoline gene in prior and current work.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: precursor of the anticancer alkaloids vincristine and vinblastine
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LLM domain B-GATA transcription factor; class IV zinc finger GATA family
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in aerial parts/leaves; rapidly induced by white light and peaked at 4 h; induced by red light and reduced after far-red treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in aerial parts/leaves; rapidly induced by white light and peaked at 4 h; induced by red light and reduced after far-red treatment
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: light; red/far-red light; phytochrome pathway; repression by CrPIF1 in the dark
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates promoters of T16H2, T3O, T3R, D4H, and DAT; overexpression up-regulates mainly T16H2, D4H, and DAT; silencing reduces T3O, T3R, and DAT
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: light-induced positive regulator required for normal vindoline accumulation in leaves/seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes vindoline accumulation and reduces tabersonine accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression in C. roseus seedlings; VIGS in leaves; promoter transactivation assays in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter mutagenesis of D4H GATC motifs; RT-qPCR; LC-MS/MS alkaloid profiling; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: continuous white light; red light followed by far-red light; dark conditions
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native T16H2, T3O, T3R, D4H, DAT promoters in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: transcriptome coexpression analysis using C. roseus transcriptomic resource SRA030483
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate positive regulator for metabolic engineering to elevate vindoline, potentially useful for increasing downstream vinblastine/vincristine precursor supply; may be combined with other vindoline regulators because not all pathway genes respond equally
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrGATA1 is a light-inducible, leaf-preferential LLM-domain B-GATA TF in C. roseus. 2. It activates promoters of five light-responsive vindoline pathway genes, with D4H activation depending on GATC motifs. 3. Overexpression increases vindoline and decreases tabersonine, whereas VIGS lowers vindoline and represses key pathway genes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Does not significantly affect upstream TIA genes TDC and STR. Evidence indicates CrGATA1 directly transactivates vindoline-pathway promoters, but some target genes show partial or context-dependent responses, implying additional regulators.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: precursor of the anticancer alkaloids vincristine and vinblastine
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Phytochrome Interacting Factor; bHLH transcription factor with phytochrome B-binding motif and bipartite nuclear localization signal
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Light responsive; functions predominantly under dark conditions; transcript affected by light
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Light responsive; functions predominantly under dark conditions; transcript affected by light
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: dark; red/far-red light via phytochrome; likely degraded in light through the 26S/ubiquitin proteasome system
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses CrGATA1 and DAT promoter activity by binding G-box/PBE-box; represses T16H2 promoter likely through E-box-like motifs; overexpression suppresses CrGATA1 and five light-responsive vindoline pathway genes in the dark
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: upstream dark-acting repressor linking phytochrome signaling to vindoline production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: reduces vindoline accumulation and increases tabersonine accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression in seedlings; VIGS in leaves; promoter transactivation assays in Nicotiana benthamiana; site-directed mutagenesis of CrGATA1 G-box and DAT PBE-box; RT-qPCR; LC-MS/MS alkaloid profiling; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: phytochrome (inferred from PIF identity/pathway, not directly tested in this paper)
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dark conditions; white light; red light followed by far-red light
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native CrGATA1, T16H2, T3O, T3R, D4H, DAT promoters in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: repression or editing of CrPIF1 could derepress vindoline biosynthesis under low-light/dark contexts; potentially useful in engineering increased vindoline precursor pools
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrPIF1 acts as a negative regulator of vindoline biosynthesis in C. roseus. 2. It represses CrGATA1 and DAT directly through G-box/PBE-box-containing promoters and suppresses pathway gene expression in the dark. 3. Overexpression decreases vindoline and increases tabersonine, whereas VIGS elevates CrGATA1, pathway-gene expression, and vindoline.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among three tested C. roseus PIFs, only CrPIF1 showed a clear effect on vindoline pathway regulation; CrPIF3 and CrPIF4/5 were not core functional regulators in the reported assays.
